edges.description: Localized proliferation yields a non-invasive circumscribed tubal or paratubal lesion.
edges.source: Localized benign epithelial or smooth muscle proliferation in tubal/adnexal tissue

edges.description: Selected lesions containing hormonally active stromal elements may perturb reproductive endocrine signaling.
edges.source: Localized benign epithelial or smooth muscle proliferation in tubal/adnexal tissue
edges.target: Hypothalamic-pituitary-ovarian axis dysregulation in selected adenofibromas

edges.description: Continued localized growth can expand the lesion and distort adjacent adnexal anatomy.
Large confined lesions become clinically appreciable as abdominal or adnexal masses.
Expanding lesions impose pressure and stretch on adnexal tissues.
edges.target: Local adnexal pressure and tissue stretch

edges.description: Anatomically visible lesions enter imaging and operative diagnostic workflows.
edges.target: Clinical detection by imaging, surgery, or pathology

edges.description: Mechanical irritation from the enlarged adnexal lesion can manifest as pelvic pain.
edges.source: Local adnexal pressure and tissue stretch

edges.description: Reproductive endocrine dysregulation can present as menstrual irregularity.
edges.source: Hypothalamic-pituitary-ovarian axis dysregulation in selected adenofibromas

nodes.description: Benign fallopian tube tumors may be detected as an incidental abdominal or adnexal mass.

evidence.reference_title: Giant serous adenofibroma of the fallopian tube.
evidence.snippet: This report presents an atypical case of a 17-year-old girl with a tubal serous adenofibroma that presented with a palpable mass occupying the entire abdomen accompanied by urinary symptoms.

nodes.description: Menstrual irregularity can occur in selected adenofibroma presentations.

evidence.reference_title: Disordered hypothalamus-pituitary-ovary axis in heterotopic extraovarian sex cord-stromal proliferation: a case report of fallopian tube serous adenofibroma
evidence.snippet: A 23-year-old woman with normal secondary sexual characters and 46, XX karyotype, presented to the gynecology clinic complaining of irregular menstrual cycles.

nodes.description: Benign tubal lesions develop papillary, cystic, fibrous, or solid architecture while remaining well circumscribed and non-destructive.

evidence.reference_title: Disordered hypothalamus-pituitary-ovary axis in heterotopic extraovarian sex cord-stromal proliferation: a case report of fallopian tube serous adenofibroma
evidence.snippet: Small cysts of 0.5–3 cm in diameter are mostly incidentally found at the fimbriae end, with coarse papillary excrescences lined by epithelial cells and connective tissue stroma without nuclear pleomorphism or mitosis.

evidence.reference_title: Paratubal Leiomyoma Mimicking Ovarian Malignancy: A Case Report and Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: A diagnostic laparoscopy revealed a well-circumscribed solid mass arising from the mesosalpinx, separate from the ovary and fallopian tube and consistent with a paratubal mass, which was successfully excised laparoscopically.

nodes.description: Most lesions are identified through pelvic imaging, operative evaluation, or histopathologic examination after resection.
nodes.id: Clinical detection by imaging, surgery, or pathology

evidence.reference_title: A case of fallopian tube adenofibroma: difficulties associated with differentiation from ectopic pregnancy.
evidence.snippet: From histopathological findings, the lesion was identified as a serous fallopian tube adenofibroma.

evidence.reference_title: Paratubal Leiomyoma Mimicking Ovarian Malignancy: A Case Report and Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: This case highlights the diagnostic challenge of differentiating paratubal leiomyomas from ovarian tumors based on imaging alone. Histopathological examination is essential for confirmation.

nodes.description: In uncommon adenofibroma presentations with heterotopic sex cord-stromal elements, altered endocrine signaling can disrupt reproductive cycling.
nodes.id: Hypothalamic-pituitary-ovarian axis dysregulation in selected adenofibromas

evidence.reference_title: Disordered hypothalamus-pituitary-ovary axis in heterotopic extraovarian sex cord-stromal proliferation: a case report of fallopian tube serous adenofibroma
evidence.snippet: The clinical picture derived from heterotopic extraovarian sex cord-stromal proliferation indicated a disordered hypothalamus-pituitary-ovary axis.

nodes.description: Distortion and stretching of local adnexal tissues create a mechanical basis for pain or discomfort in symptomatic cases.
nodes.id: Local adnexal pressure and tissue stretch

evidence.reference_title: The fallopian tube and its pathology: Paratubal cysts, tubal torsion, and pelvic inflammatory disease.
evidence.snippet: A thorough history and abdominal examination should be performed and may help delineate adnexal etiologies of pain or discomfort.

nodes.description: Benign epithelial-stromal or smooth muscle proliferation arises in the fallopian tube or adjacent paratubal tissue and remains spatially confined rather than adopting invasive malignant behavior.
nodes.id: Localized benign epithelial or smooth muscle proliferation in tubal/adnexal tissue

evidence.reference_title: Giant serous adenofibroma of the fallopian tube.
evidence.snippet: Serous adenofibroma of the fallopian tube is a rare, benign tumour of the female genital tract.

evidence.reference_title: Paratubal Leiomyoma Mimicking Ovarian Malignancy: A Case Report and Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: A paratubal leiomyoma is an exceptionally rare benign smooth muscle tumor arising from paratubal tissue, with only sporadic cases reported in the literature.

nodes.description: Local adnexal distortion can cause intermittent pelvic pain.

evidence.reference_title: The fallopian tube and its pathology: Paratubal cysts, tubal torsion, and pelvic inflammatory disease.
evidence.snippet: A thorough history and abdominal examination should be performed and may help delineate adnexal etiologies of pain or discomfort.

nodes.description: Ongoing enlargement of a confined tubal or paratubal lesion can expand the adnexa, occupy abdominal space, and distort local anatomy without invasion.

evidence.reference_title: Giant serous adenofibroma of the fallopian tube.
evidence.snippet: This report presents an atypical case of a 17-year-old girl with a tubal serous adenofibroma that presented with a palpable mass occupying the entire abdomen accompanied by urinary symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: Paratubal Leiomyoma Mimicking Ovarian Malignancy: A Case Report and Literature Review.
evidence.snippet: A transvaginal ultrasound demonstrated a complex left adnexal mass with calcifications, and computed tomography (CT) confirmed a 7.8 × 5.5 × 4.7 cm lesion suggestive of an ovarian malignancy.
